title: Do you mind the color of LCL
display_title: Do you mind the color of LCL
wikitext:
[]: Do you mind the color of LCL?             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Tue Feb 15, 2011 3:06 pm The "Everyone turns to tang!" jokes are all well and good, but they kind of indicate that perhaps orange makes the beginning of Instrumentality a little hard to take seriously. I've seen some people express that sentiment, at least. I certainly don't think it's a big deal, but is there a color you think would have worked better? Red would have looked too much like blood, so no, but black/grey/white? Orange has a certain biological-but-alien feel that's fitting...

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Feb 15, 2011 4:44 pm Well the color it is has always made me think the lcl tastes like sweet and sour sauce for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Aozora » Tue Feb 15, 2011 4:53 pm Well, I never had a problem with the color. The brightness makes me think of it as fresh and active (compared to the murky stagnated color it is in Rebuild). I imagine it would taste vaguely unpleasant but easy to adjust to and ignore.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 15, 2011 4:55 pm it just fits well into the colour schema. Certainly much better than white or black

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 15, 2011 5:17 pm I always thought it was meant to look like blood plasma or diluted blood... Blood actually turns orange or yellow if you mix it with water, the shade depending on the mixture ratio. Look here: the left pic shows the components of blood. The orange-ish stuff is blood plasma, the liquid with the cells filtered out. The chemical makeup of the LCL in Shinji's entry plug changes after he dissolves, so i recon that the LCL used in the entryplug is somehow 'filtered' (There's a filtering system mentioned in ep 16), maybe to rid it of some components WITH those components, it's said to resemble the soup of life. Most of us tend to associate 'blood' and 'life'. The unfiltered LCL is 'real blood', and the filtered stuff is, in a way, just the plasma. Or maybe they just wanted it to look like something blood related, but not obviously so. Of course, it was still orange and not red when we were shown how it pour out of lillith, but this is most likely just so we recognize it's the same stuff. Or they just didn't take the analogy that far. However, with all the 'biological' terms apearing in NGE, it's not that far a leap to assume that the color of LCL was at least inspired by blood plasma. As for the taste: I always imagined it to be metallic (like blood) and slightly salty like tears.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Feb 15, 2011 5:21 pm I'd have thrown a human in a blender, and used whatever colour the end result was.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Feb 15, 2011 5:25 pm I like it because it stands out easily. Well, that and the fact that it makes for good Tang jokes.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 15, 2011 5:25 pm Orange is a very serious color.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:01 pm I would have probably made it neon blue or something if I made the series but I like the orange, it stands out well.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:02 pm I can't imagine it in any other color. Green, purple, pink, brown...ugh, this reminds me of various body fluids.

[]:             Quote Post by bigbadtyrant » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:30 pm LCL just seems like it should be orange to me. If it was red, it would look too much like blood. If it was yellow, thats just setting the base for many innappropriate jokes (worse than the tang jokes). Green, blue, purple, black, white ,(clear?), i feel none of those would work. I think that orange works best for what i feel LCL should look like.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:54 pm I would have gone for deep blue and my-god-it's-full-of-stars myself. It would have been so pretty.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 15, 2011 8:36 pm It’s not something I’ve ever thought about, but I guess I don’t mind the color.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:20 pm Xard wrote: it just fits well into the colour schema. Certainly much better than white or black

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:24 pm Sachi wrote: Orange is a very serious color.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:26 pm ^And yet another secret of Eva is revealed.

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:53 pm Man, I want me a glass of LCL now.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Feb 15, 2011 11:10 pm child of Lilith wrote: ^And yet another secret of Eva is revealed.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Tue Feb 15, 2011 11:35 pm Actually, the color of LCL does bother me, but only because it can't seem to make up its mind whether it's red or orange. Personally, I prefer orange.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Tue Feb 15, 2011 11:41 pm Better than seeing Red. Orange is not a bad color for the use of LCL. It puts you in a calm state.

I've seen some people express that sentiment, at least. I certainly don't think it's a big deal, but is there a color you think would have worked b

Article: Do you mind the color of LCL
[]: Do you mind the color of LCL?             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Tue Feb 15, 2011 3:06 pm The "Everyone turns to tang!" jokes are all well and good, but they kind of indicate that perhaps orange makes the beginning of Instrumentality a little hard to take seriously. I've seen some people express that sentiment, at least. I certainly don't think it's a big deal, but is there a color you think would have worked better? Red would have looked too much like blood, so no, but black/grey/white? Orange has a certain biological-but-alien feel that's fitting...

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Feb 15, 2011 4:44 pm Well the color it is has always made me think the lcl tastes like sweet and sour sauce for some reason.

The brightness makes me think of it as fresh and active (compared to the murky stagnated color it is in Rebuild). I imagine it would taste vaguely unpleasant but easy to adjust to and ignore.
